/*
Package applyconfigurations provides typesafe go representations of the apply
configurations that are used to constructs Server-side Apply requests.

# Basics

The Apply functions in the typed client (see the k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed packages) offer
a direct and typesafe way of calling Server-side Apply. Each Apply function takes an "apply
configuration" type as an argument, which is a structured representation of an Apply request. For
example:

        import (
             ...
             v1ac "k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/autoscaling/v1"
        )
        hpaApplyConfig := v1ac.HorizontalPodAutoscaler(autoscalerName, ns).
             WithSpec(v1ac.HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec().
                      WithMinReplicas(0)
             )
        return hpav1client.Apply(ctx, hpaApplyConfig, metav1.ApplyOptions{FieldManager: "mycontroller", Force: true})

Note in this example that HorizontalPodAutoscaler is imported from an "applyconfigurations"
package. Each "apply configuration" type represents the same Kubernetes object kind as the
corresponding go struct, but where all fields are pointers to make them optional, allowing apply
requests to be accurately represented. For example, this when the apply configuration in the above
example is marshalled to YAML, it produces:

        apiVersion: autoscaling/v1
        kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
        metadata:
            name: myHPA
            namespace: myNamespace
        spec:
            minReplicas: 0

To understand why this is needed, the above YAML cannot be produced by the
v1.HorizontalPodAutoscaler go struct. Take for example:

        hpa := v1.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{
             TypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{
                      APIVersion: "autoscaling/v1",
                      Kind:       "HorizontalPodAutoscaler",
             },
             ObjectMeta: ObjectMeta{
                      Namespace: ns,
                      Name:      autoscalerName,
             },
             Spec: v1.HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec{
                      MinReplicas: pointer.Int32Ptr(0),
             },
        }

The above code attempts to declare the same apply configuration as shown in the previous examples,
but when marshalled to YAML, produces:

        kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
        apiVersion: autoscaling/v1
        metadata:
          name: myHPA
          namespace: myNamespace
        spec:
          scaleTargetRef:
            kind: ""
            name: ""
          minReplicas: 0
          maxReplicas: 0

Which, among other things, contains spec.maxReplicas set to 0. This is almost certainly not what
the caller intended (the intended apply configuration says nothing about the maxReplicas field),
and could have serious consequences on a production system: it directs the autoscaler to downscale
to zero pods. The problem here originates from the fact that the go structs contain required fields
that are zero valued if not set explicitly. The go structs work as intended for create and update
operations, but are fundamentally incompatible with apply, which is why we have introduced the
generated "apply configuration" types.

The "apply configurations" also have convenience With<FieldName> functions that make it easier to
build apply requests. This allows developers to set fields without having to deal with the fact that
all the fields in the "apply configuration" types are pointers, and are inconvenient to set using
go. For example "MinReplicas: &0" is not legal go code, so without the With functions, developers
would work around this problem by using a library, .e.g. "MinReplicas: pointer.Int32Ptr(0)", but
string enumerations like corev1.Protocol are still a problem since they cannot be supported by a
general purpose library. In addition to the convenience, the With functions also isolate
developers from the underlying representation, which makes it safer for the underlying
representation to be changed to support additional features in the future.

# Controller Support

The new client-go support makes it much easier to use Server-side Apply in controllers, by either of
two mechanisms.

Mechanism 1:

When authoring new controllers to use Server-side Apply, a good approach is to have the controller
recreate the apply configuration for an object each time it reconciles that object.  This ensures
that the controller fully reconciles all the fields that it is responsible for. Controllers
typically should unconditionally set all the fields they own by setting "Force: true" in the
ApplyOptions. Controllers must also provide a FieldManager name that is unique to the
reconciliation loop that apply is called from.

When upgrading existing controllers to use Server-side Apply the same approach often works
well--migrate the controllers to recreate the apply configuration each time it reconciles any
object. For cases where this does not work well, see Mechanism 2.

Mechanism 2:

When upgrading existing controllers to use Server-side Apply, the controller might have multiple
code paths that update different parts of an object depending on various conditions. Migrating a
controller like this to Server-side Apply can be risky because if the controller forgets to include
any fields in an apply configuration that is included in a previous apply request, a field can be
accidentally deleted. For such cases, an alternative to mechanism 1 is to replace any controller
reconciliation code that performs a "read/modify-in-place/update" (or patch) workflow with a
"extract/modify-in-place/apply" workflow. Here's an example of the new workflow:

            fieldMgr := "my-field-manager"
            deploymentClient := clientset.AppsV1().Deployments("default")
            // read, could also be read from a shared informer
            deployment, err := deploymentClient.Get(ctx, "example-deployment", metav1.GetOptions{})
            if err != nil {
              // handle error
            }
            // extract
            deploymentApplyConfig, err := appsv1ac.ExtractDeployment(deployment, fieldMgr)
            if err != nil {
              // handle error
            }
            // modify-in-place
            deploymentApplyConfig.Spec.Template.Spec.WithContainers(corev1ac.Container().
                WithName("modify-slice").
                WithImage("nginx:1.14.2"),
            )
            // apply
            applied, err := deploymentClient.Apply(ctx, extractedDeployment, metav1.ApplyOptions{FieldManager: fieldMgr})

## Complete Source Knowledge Base

### apply.go (client-go/util/apply/apply.go)
- `NewRequest(client, applyConfiguration)`: marshals to JSON (or CBOR if `ClientsAllowCBOR`+`ClientsPreferCBOR` feature gates enabled), sets Content-Type to `ApplyYAMLPatchType` or `ApplyCBORPatchType`, returns `client.Patch(pt).Body(body)`

### Decorator Chain (outermost→innermost) — confirmed in fieldmanager.go
```
versionCheckManager
  → lastAppliedUpdater
    → lastAppliedManager
      → skipNonAppliedManager (probabilistic)
        → capManagersManager
          → buildManagerInfoManager
            → managedFieldsUpdater
              → stripMetaManager
                → structuredMergeManager  ← innermost
```
Constructed in `NewDefaultFieldManager()` in `internal/fieldmanager.go`

### Per-decorator behavior (all confirmed):

**versionCheckManager** (`versioncheck.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: validates `appliedObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind() == f.gvk`; returns `errors.NewBadRequest` if mismatch

**lastAppliedUpdater** (`lastappliedupdater.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: after inner Apply succeeds, if `manager == "kubectl"` AND `hasLastApplied(liveObj)`, calls `buildLastApplied(newObj)` → `SetLastApplied(liveObj, value)` to sync `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation

**lastAppliedManager** (`lastappliedmanager.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: calls inner Apply; if `manager == "kubectl"` and conflicts occur, checks `allowedConflictsFromLastApplied(liveObj)` — reads annotation, converts to typed, computes field set minus modified/added/removed fields; if all conflicts are in allowed set, re-applies with `force=true`; otherwise returns reduced conflict set

**skipNonAppliedManager** (`skipnonapplied.go`):
- `NewSkipNonAppliedManager`: p=0.0 (never track on create)
- `NewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager`: configurable p
- Update: if `managed.Fields()` empty AND UID empty (create) → skip if `p <= rand.Float32()`; if UID present (update) → always skip; else delegate
- Apply: if `managed.Fields()` empty → creates empty object of same GVK, calls `fieldManager.Update(emptyObj, liveObj, managed, "before-first-apply")` to bootstrap field tracking, then delegates Apply

**capManagersManager** (`capmanagers.go`):
- Update: delegates, then calls `capUpdateManagers(managed)`
- Apply: pass-through
- `capUpdateManagers`: collects all non-applied (update) managers; if count ≤ `maxUpdateManagers` (10), no-op; else sorts by time (oldest first), merges oldest into versioned buckets named `"ancient-changes"` with `BuildManagerIdentifier`; merges field sets with Union; decrements count until ≤ cap

**buildManagerInfoManager** (`buildmanagerinfo.go`):
- Update: builds manager identifier JSON: `{manager, Operation:Update, APIVersion, Subresource}`; empty manager → "unknown"
- Apply: builds manager identifier JSON: `{manager, Operation:Apply, Subresource}` (no APIVersion for apply)

**managedFieldsUpdater** (`managedfieldsupdater.go`):
- Update: delegates with synthetic manager `"current-operation"`; if that key exists in result fields → deletes it, merges with existing `manager` entry (Union), sets `managed.Times()[manager] = time.Now().UTC()`
- Apply: delegates; if result non-nil → sets `managed.Times()[fieldManager] = time.Now().UTC()`; if nil → deep-copies liveObj, removes managed fields

**stripMetaManager** (`stripmeta.go`):
- Both Update and Apply: after delegation, removes from manager's field set: `apiVersion`, `kind`, `metadata`, `metadata.name`, `metadata.namespace`, `metadata.creationTimestamp`, `metadata.selfLink`, `metadata.uid`, `metadata.clusterName`, `metadata.generation`, `metadata.managedFields`, `metadata.resourceVersion`
- If resulting set empty → deletes manager entry entirely

**structuredMergeManager** (`structuredmerge.go`) — innermost:
- Update: converts both objects to versioned GV, converts to `typed.TypedValue` (with `AllowDuplicates`), calls `f.updater.Update(liveObjTyped, newObjTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)` → returns newObj with updated managedFields
- Apply: validates patch GV matches; validates `patchObj.GetManagedFields() == nil`; converts live to versioned; converts patch to typed (no AllowDuplicates — rejects duplicates); calls `f.updater.Apply(liveObjTyped, patchObjTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager, force)`; if `newObjTyped == nil` → no-op; else: `TypedToObject` → `toVersioned` → `objectDefaulter.Default()` → `toUnversioned`
- `updater` is `merge.Updater{Converter: versionConverter, IgnoreFilter: resetFields}`

### FieldManager public API (`internal/fieldmanager.go`):
- `Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls `decodeLiveOrNew()`, `RemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)`, delegates to chain, `EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed)`
- `UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: same but on error logs at most once/second via `atMostEverySecond`, clears managedFields, returns newObj
- `Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, manager, force)`: decodes managed from liveObj only; delegates; converts `merge.Conflicts` → `NewConflictError`; encodes result
- `decodeLiveOrNew()`: subresource → use live's managedFields; `isResetManagedFields` (non-nil empty list or single empty entry) → `NewEmptyManaged()`; else try new object's fields, fall back to live on error/empty
- `isResetManagedFields`: true if `len==0 && managedFields != nil`, or `len==1 && reflect.DeepEqual(entry, ManagedFieldsEntry{})`

### ManagedInterface (`managedfields.go`):
- `Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields` — map[string]VersionedSet
- `Times() map[string]*metav1.Time`
- `BuildManagerIdentifier`: JSON of entry minus FieldsType, FieldsV1, Time; for Apply also minus APIVersion
- `DecodeManagedFields`: validates Operation (Apply|Update), APIVersion non-empty, FieldsType=="FieldsV1"
- `encodeManagedFields`: sorts by (Operation, Time, Manager, APIVersion, Subresource)
- `EmptyFields = metav1.FieldsV1{Raw: []byte("{}")}`

### TypeConverter (`internal/typeconverter.go`):
- `NewTypeConverter(openapiSpec, preserveUnknownFields)`: `schemaconv.ToSchemaFromOpenAPI` → `typed.Parser` → `indexModels` (maps GVK→ParseableType via `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` extension)
- `NewDeducedTypeConverter()`: for CRDs without schema; all fields atomic; uses `typed.DeducedParseableType`
- `ObjectToTyped`: dispatch on `*unstructured.Unstructured` vs structured
- `TypedToObject`: `value.Unstructured()` → `map[string]interface{}` → `*unstructured.Unstructured`

### versionConverter (`versionconverter.go`):
- Implements `merge.Converter`
- `Convert(object, version)`: TypedToObject → ConvertToVersion(hub) → ConvertToVersion(target) → ObjectToTyped
- Built-in: `hubGetter = func(from) { Group: from.Group, Version: hub.Version }`
- CRD: `hubGetter = func(from) { return hub }` (fixed hub GV)
- `IsMissingVersionError`: `runtime.IsNotRegisteredError || isNoCorrespondingTypeError`

### Conflict handling:
- `NewConflictError(conflicts)` in `internal/conflict.go`: creates `StatusCause{Type: CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict, Message: "conflict with <manager>", Field: path}` per conflict; calls `errors.NewApplyConflict(causes, message)` → HTTP 409
- `printManager`: unmarshals manager JSON, formats as `"name" using apiVersion [at time]` for updates, just `"name"` for apply

### patch.go handler (apiserver):
- `applyPatcher.applyPatchToCurrentObject`: unmarshals patch bytes into `*unstructured.Unstructured` via `unmarshalFn`; calls `p.fieldManager.Apply(obj, patchObj, p.options.FieldManager, force)`; if `validationDirective==Strict/Warn`, also runs `unmarshalStrictFn` for unknown field detection
- `applyPatcher.createNewObject`: `p.creater.New(p.kind)` → `applyPatchToCurrentObject`
- Too-large retry (non-apply only): adds transformer that calls `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` and retries

### ExtractInto (`extract.go`):
- Finds `ManagedFieldsEntry` where `Manager==fieldManager && Operation==Apply && Subresource==subresource`
- Decodes `FieldsV1.Raw` → `fieldpath.Set`
- Calls `typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves()).AsValue().Unstructured()`
- Injects kind/apiVersion if missing
- `runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(m, applyConfiguration)`

### ScaleHandler (`scalehandler.go`):
- `ToSubresource()`: filters parent entries to those with known version mapping AND having replicas path; remaps to `spec.replicas` in `autoscaling/v1`
- `ToParent(scaleEntries)`: merges scale entries back, translating `spec.replicas` path back to resource-specific path; handles "stolen" replicas ownership

### lastApplied (`lastapplied.go`):
- `LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration"`
- `SetLastApplied`: sets annotation; if `ValidateAnnotationsSize` fails → silently drops annotation

### AtMostEvery (`atmostevery.go`):
- Thread-safe rate limiter; `Do(fn)` runs fn at most once per `delay`; used for error logging in `UpdateNoErrors`

### client-go applyconfigurations:
- `internal/internal.go`: 15,478-line generated file; `Parser()` singleton returns `typed.Parser` from embedded `schemaYAML` (SMD schema for all built-in types)
- `utils.go`: `ForKind(gvk)` giant switch returning typed `*XxxApplyConfiguration{}`
- `gentype/type.go`: `alsoApplier[T,C].Apply()` validates obj non-nil + name set, calls `apply.NewRequest`, chains REST request params
- `applyconfiguration-gen`: generates both the typed structs and the embedded schema YAML

### installer.go (apiserver):
- `ResetFieldsStrategy` vs `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy` mutual exclusion
- Calls `managedfields.NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter, unsafeConvertor, defaulter, creater, fqKind, hubGV, subresource, resetFieldsFilter)`

### Constants:
- `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers = 10`
- `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability = 1` (float32)
- `atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)`

### Patch types (`types/patch.go`):
- `ApplyYAMLPatchType = "application/apply-patch+yaml"`
- `ApplyCBORPatchType = "application/apply-patch+cbor"`
- `JSONPatchType`, `MergePatchType`, `StrategicMergePatchType`

## Complete Source Knowledge Base

### apply.go (client-go/util/apply/apply.go) — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- `NewRequest(client, applyConfiguration)`: checks `ClientsAllowCBOR && ClientsPreferCBOR` feature gates; if both enabled → `cbor.Marshal` + `ApplyCBORPatchType`; else `json.Marshal` + `ApplyYAMLPatchType`; returns `client.Patch(pt).Body(body)`

### gentype/type.go — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- `alsoApplier[T,C].Apply()`: validates obj non-nil + `obj.GetName() != nil`; calls `apply.NewRequest(client, obj)`; chains `.UseProtobufAsDefaultIfPreferred().NamespaceIfScoped().Resource().Name().VersionedParams(&patchOpts).Do(ctx).Into(result)`
- `alsoApplier[T,C].ApplyStatus()`: same pattern but adds `.SubResource("status")`
- `NewClientWithApply` / `NewClientWithListAndApply`: constructors embedding `alsoApplier`

### managerOrUserAgent (create.go:259) — CONFIRMED
- `managerOrUserAgent(manager, userAgent string)`: if manager != "" → return manager; else `prefixFromUserAgent(userAgent)` (splits on "/", trims to FieldManagerMaxLength)

### installer.go (apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go) — CONFIRMED
- Lines 697-724: checks storage for `ResetFieldsStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`) OR `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`); mutual exclusion enforced; calls `fieldpath.NewExcludeFilterSetMap()` for former; passes `resetFieldsFilter` to `managedfields.NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter, unsafeConvertor, defaulter, creater, fqKind, hubGV, subresource, resetFieldsFilter)`

### rest/rest.go — CONFIRMED
- `ResetFieldsStrategy`: `GetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`
- `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy`: `GetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`
- `CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy` = `RESTCreateUpdateStrategy + ResetFieldsStrategy`
- `UpdateResetFieldsStrategy` = `RESTUpdateStrategy + ResetFieldsStrategy`

### patch.go (apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go) — CONFIRMED
- Line 140-141: `ApplyCBORPatchType` gated on `CBORServingAndStorage` feature gate
- Line 372: non-apply patches → `UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, managerOrUserAgent(...))`
- Line 466: strategic/JSON/merge patches → `UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, managerOrUserAgent(...))`
- Line 713: `isTooLargeError` retry only for non-apply patch types

### gvkparser.go — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- `GvkParser`: holds `gvks map[schema.GroupVersionKind]string` + `parser typed.Parser`
- `NewGVKParser(models proto.Models, preserveUnknownFields bool)`: calls `schemaconv.ToSchemaWithPreserveUnknownFields(models, preserveUnknownFields)` → builds `typed.Parser{Schema: smdschema.Schema{Types: typeSchema.Types}}`; iterates `models.ListModels()`, calls `parseGroupVersionKind(model)` reading `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` extension; maps GVK → modelName
- `Type(gvk)`: looks up typeName in gvks map, returns `&parser.Type(typeName)`
- `parseGroupVersionKind`: reads `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` extension as `[]interface{}` of `map[interface{}]interface{}`

### typeconverter.go (public API) — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- `TypeConverter = internal.TypeConverter` (type alias)
- `NewDeducedTypeConverter()` → `internal.NewDeducedTypeConverter()` (for CRDs without schema; all fields atomic)
- `NewTypeConverter(openapiSpec map[string]*spec.Schema, preserveUnknownFields bool)` → `internal.NewTypeConverter(...)`
- `NewSchemeTypeConverter(scheme *runtime.Scheme, parser *typed.Parser)` → `internal.NewSchemeTypeConverter(...)` (new in 2025)

### runtimetypeconverter.go (internal) — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- `schemeTypeConverter{scheme, parser}`: implements `TypeConverter`
- `ObjectToTyped`: calls `scheme.ToOpenAPIDefinitionName(gvk)` → `parser.Type(name)` → `t.FromUnstructured()` or `t.FromStructured()`
- `TypedToObject`: `value.AsValue().Unstructured()` → wraps in `*unstructured.Unstructured`

### applyconfigurations/core/v1/pod.go — CONFIRMED
- `PodApplyConfiguration`: all fields are pointers; embeds `TypeMetaApplyConfiguration` inline, `*ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration` with omitempty
- `Pod(name, namespace string)`: constructor sets Name, Namespace, Kind="Pod", APIVersion="v1"
- `ExtractPodFrom(pod, fieldManager, subresource)`: calls `managedfields.ExtractInto(pod, internal.Parser().Type("io.k8s.api.core.v1.Pod"), fieldManager, b, subresource)`; then sets Name, Namespace, Kind, APIVersion
- `ExtractPod(pod, fieldManager)` → `ExtractPodFrom(pod, fieldManager, "")`
- `ExtractPodEphemeralcontainers(pod, fieldManager)` → `ExtractPodFrom(pod, fieldManager, "ephemeralcontainers")`

### applyconfigurations/meta/v1/objectmeta.go — CONFIRMED
- `ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration`: all fields are `*T` pointers with `json:",omitempty"` tags

### applyconfigurations/doc.go — CONFIRMED FULL READ
- Documents two controller mechanisms:
  - Mechanism 1: Recreate full apply config each reconcile; use `Force: true`; unique FieldManager per loop
  - Mechanism 2: Extract/modify-in-place/apply workflow for controllers with conditional update paths
- Explains why pointer-based structs are needed (zero-value problem with regular Go structs)

### applyconfigurations/utils.go — CONFIRMED
- `ForKind(gvk schema.GroupVersionKind) interface{}`: giant switch over all built-in GVKs returning typed `*XxxApplyConfiguration{}`; used by `ExtractInto` for dynamic dispatch

### Decorator Chain (outermost→innermost) — confirmed in fieldmanager.go
```
versionCheckManager
  → lastAppliedUpdater
    → lastAppliedManager
      → skipNonAppliedManager (probabilistic)
        → capManagersManager
          → buildManagerInfoManager
            → managedFieldsUpdater
              → stripMetaManager
                → structuredMergeManager  ← innermost
```
Constructed in `NewDefaultFieldManager()` in `internal/fieldmanager.go`

### Per-decorator behavior (all confirmed):

**versionCheckManager** (`versioncheck.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: validates `appliedObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind() == f.gvk`; returns `errors.NewBadRequest` if mismatch

**lastAppliedUpdater** (`lastappliedupdater.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: after inner Apply succeeds, if `manager == "kubectl"` AND `hasLastApplied(liveObj)`, calls `buildLastApplied(newObj)` → `SetLastApplied(liveObj, value)` to sync `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation

**lastAppliedManager** (`lastappliedmanager.go`):
- Update: pass-through
- Apply: calls inner Apply; if `manager == "kubectl"` and conflicts occur, checks `allowedConflictsFromLastApplied(liveObj)` — reads annotation, converts to typed, computes field set minus modified/added/removed fields; if all conflicts are in allowed set, re-applies with `force=true`; otherwise returns reduced conflict set

**skipNonAppliedManager** (`skipnonapplied.go`):
- `NewSkipNonAppliedManager`: p=0.0 (never track on create)
- `NewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager`: configurable p
- Update: if `managed.Fields()` empty AND UID empty (create) → skip if `p <= rand.Float32()`; if UID present (update) → always skip; else delegate
- Apply: if `managed.Fields()` empty → creates empty object of same GVK, calls `fieldManager.Update(emptyObj, liveObj, managed, "before-first-apply")` to bootstrap field tracking, then delegates Apply

**capManagersManager** (`capmanagers.go`):
- Update: delegates, then calls `capUpdateManagers(managed)`
- Apply: pass-through
- `capUpdateManagers`: collects all non-applied (update) managers; if count ≤ `maxUpdateManagers` (10), no-op; else sorts by time (oldest first), merges oldest into versioned buckets named `"ancient-changes"` with `BuildManagerIdentifier`; merges field sets with Union; decrements count until ≤ cap

**buildManagerInfoManager** (`buildmanagerinfo.go`):
- Update: builds manager identifier JSON: `{manager, Operation:Update, APIVersion, Subresource}`; empty manager → "unknown"
- Apply: builds manager identifier JSON: `{manager, Operation:Apply, Subresource}` (no APIVersion for apply)

**managedFieldsUpdater** (`managedfieldsupdater.go`):
- Update: delegates with synthetic manager `"current-operation"`; if that key exists in result fields → deletes it, merges with existing `manager` entry (Union), sets `managed.Times()[manager] = time.Now().UTC()`
- Apply: delegates; if result non-nil → sets `managed.Times()[fieldManager] = time.Now().UTC()`; if nil → deep-copies liveObj, removes managed fields

**stripMetaManager** (`stripmeta.go`):
- Both Update and Apply: after delegation, removes from manager's field set: `apiVersion`, `kind`, `metadata`, `metadata.name`, `metadata.namespace`, `metadata.creationTimestamp`, `metadata.selfLink`, `metadata.uid`, `metadata.clusterName`, `metadata.generation`, `metadata.managedFields`, `metadata.resourceVersion`
- If resulting set empty → deletes manager entry entirely

**structuredMergeManager** (`structuredmerge.go`) — innermost:
- Update: converts liveObj + newObj to typed via TypeConverter; calls `comparison.Comparison()` to compute field ownership; calls `updater.Update(liveTyped, newTyped, version, managed)` from sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff
- Apply: converts appliedObj to typed; calls `updater.Apply(liveTyped, appliedTyped, version, managed, manager, force)`; on conflict → returns `NewConflictError(conflicts)`; handles `managed fields too large` by calling `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` and retrying

### ExtractInto (`extract.go`):
- Finds `ManagedFieldsEntry` where `Manager==fieldManager && Operation==Apply && Subresource==subresource`
- Decodes `FieldsV1.Raw` → `fieldpath.Set`
- Calls `typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves()).AsValue().Unstructured()`
- Injects kind/apiVersion if missing
- `runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(m, applyConfiguration)`

### ScaleHandler (`scalehandler.go`):
- `ToSubresource()`: filters parent entries to those with known version mapping AND having replicas path; remaps to `spec.replicas` in `autoscaling/v1`
- `ToParent(scaleEntries)`: merges scale entries back, translating `spec.replicas` path back to resource-specific path; handles "stolen" replicas ownership

### lastApplied (`lastapplied.go`):
- `LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration"`
- `SetLastApplied`: sets annotation; if `ValidateAnnotationsSize` fails → silently drops annotation

### AtMostEvery (`atmostevery.go`):
- Thread-safe rate limiter; `Do(fn)` runs fn at most once per `delay`; used for error logging in `UpdateNoErrors`

### client-go applyconfigurations:
- `internal/internal.go`: 15,478-line generated file; `Parser()` singleton returns `typed.Parser` from embedded `schemaYAML` (SMD schema for all built-in types)
- `utils.go`: `ForKind(gvk)` giant switch returning typed `*XxxApplyConfiguration{}`
- `gentype/type.go`: `alsoApplier[T,C].Apply()` validates obj non-nil + name set, calls `apply.NewRequest`, chains REST request params
- `applyconfiguration-gen`: generates both the typed structs and the embedded schema YAML

### installer.go (apiserver):
- `ResetFieldsStrategy` vs `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy` mutual exclusion
- Calls `managedfields.NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter, unsafeConvertor, defaulter, creater, fqKind, hubGV, subresource, resetFieldsFilter)`

### Constants:
- `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers = 10`
- `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability = 1` (float32)
- `atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)`

### Patch types (`types/patch.go`):
- `ApplyYAMLPatchType = "application/apply-patch+yaml"`
- `ApplyCBORPatchType = "application/apply-patch+cbor"`
- `JSONPatchType`, `MergePatchType`, `StrategicMergePatchType`

/*
Package applyconfigurations provides typesafe go representations of the apply
configurations that are used to constructs Server-side Apply requests.

# Basics

The Apply functions in the typed client (see the k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed packages) offer
a direct and typesafe way of calling Server-side Apply. Each Apply function takes an "apply
configuration" type as an argument, which is a structured representation of an Apply request. For
example:

        import (
             ...
             v1ac "k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/autoscaling/v1"
        )
        hpaApplyConfig := v1ac.HorizontalPodAutoscaler(autoscalerName, ns).
             WithSpec(v1ac.HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec().
                      WithMinReplicas(0)
             )
        return hpav1client.Apply(ctx, hpaApplyConfig, metav1.ApplyOptions{FieldManager: "mycontroller", Force: true})

Note in this example that HorizontalPodAutoscaler is imported from an "applyconfigurations"
package. Each "apply configuration" type represents the same Kubernetes object kind as the
corresponding go struct, but where all fields are pointers to make them optional, allowing apply
requests to be accurately represented. For example, this when the apply configuration in the above
example is marshalled to YAML, it produces:

        apiVersion: autoscaling/v1
        kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
        metadata:
            name: myHPA
            namespace: myNamespace
        spec:
            minReplicas: 0

To understand why this is needed, the above YAML cannot be produced by the
v1.HorizontalPodAutoscaler go struct. Take for example:

        hpa := v1.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{
             TypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{
                      APIVersion: "autoscaling/v1",
                      Kind:       "HorizontalPodAutoscaler",
             },
             ObjectMeta: ObjectMeta{
                      Namespace: ns,
                      Name:      autoscalerName,
             },
             Spec: v1.HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec{
                      MinReplicas: pointer.Int32Ptr(0),
             },
        }

The above code attempts to declare the same apply configuration as shown in the previous examples,
but when marshalled to YAML, produces:

        kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
        apiVersion: autoscaling/v1
        metadata:
          name: myHPA
          namespace: myNamespace
        spec:
          scaleTargetRef:
            kind: ""
            name: ""
          minReplicas: 0
          maxReplicas: 0

Which, among other things, contains spec.maxReplicas set to 0. This is almost certainly not what
the caller intended (the intended apply configuration says nothing about the maxReplicas field),
and could have serious consequences on a production system: it directs the autoscaler to downscale
to zero pods. The problem here originates from the fact that the go structs contain required fields
that are zero valued if not set explicitly. The go structs work as intended for create and update
operations, but are fundamentally incompatible with apply, which is why we have introduced the
generated "apply configuration" types.

The "apply configurations" also have convenience With<FieldName> functions that make it easier to
build apply requests. This allows developers to set fields without having to deal with the fact that
all the fields in the "apply configuration" types are pointers, and are inconvenient to set using
go. For example "MinReplicas: &0" is not legal go code, so without the With functions, developers
would work around this problem by using a library, .e.g. "MinReplicas: pointer.Int32Ptr(0)", but
string enumerations like corev1.Protocol are still a problem since they cannot be supported by a
general purpose library. In addition to the convenience, the With functions also isolate
developers from the underlying representation, which makes it safer for the underlying
representation to be changed to support additional features in the future.

# Controller Support

The new client-go support makes it much easier to use Server-side Apply in controllers, by either of
two mechanisms.

Mechanism 1:

When authoring new controllers to use Server-side Apply, a good approach is to have the controller
recreate the apply configuration for an object each time it reconciles that object.  This ensures
that the controller fully reconciles all the fields that it is responsible for. Controllers
typically should unconditionally set all the fields they own by setting "Force: true" in the
ApplyOptions. Controllers must also provide a FieldManager name that is unique to the
reconciliation loop that apply is called from.

When upgrading existing controllers to use Server-side Apply the same approach often works
well--migrate the controllers to recreate the apply configuration each time it reconciles any
object. For cases where this does not work well, see Mechanism 2.

Mechanism 2:

When upgrading existing controllers to use Server-side Apply, the controller might have multiple
code paths that update different parts of an object depending on various conditions. Migrating a
controller like this to Server-side Apply can be risky because if the controller forgets to include
any fields in an apply configuration that is included in a previous apply request, a field can be
accidentally deleted. For such cases, an alternative to mechanism 1 is to replace any controller
reconciliation code that performs a "read/modify-in-place/update" (or patch) workflow with a
"extract/modify-in-place/apply" workflow. Here's an example of the new workflow:

            fieldMgr := "my-field-manager"
            deploymentClient := clientset.AppsV1().Deployments("default")
            // read, could also be read from a shared informer
            deployment, err := deploymentClient.Get(ctx, "example-deployment", metav1.GetOptions{})
            if err != nil {
              // handle error
            }
            // extract
            deploymentApplyConfig, err := appsv1ac.ExtractDeployment(deployment, fieldMgr)
            if err != nil {
              // handle error
            }
            // modify-in-place
            deploymentApplyConfig.Spec.Template.Spec.WithContainers(corev1ac.Container().
                WithName("modify-slice").
                WithImage("nginx:1.14.2"),
            )
            // apply
            applied, err := deploymentClient.Apply(ctx, extractedDeployment, metav1.ApplyOptions{FieldManager: fieldMgr})
*/

## Complete Source Knowledge Base

### Manager Decorator Chain (innermost → outermost)
`NewDefaultFieldManager` in `internal/fieldmanager.go` (line 57) wraps in this order:
1. `structuredMergeManager` (core SMD logic)
2. `NewStripMetaManager` — strips apiVersion, kind, metadata.name, namespace, uid, creationTimestamp, selfLink, generation, managedFields, resourceVersion from tracked fieldsets
3. `NewManagedFieldsUpdater` — uses sentinel manager "current-operation" to detect changes; updates timestamps; merges with previous entries from same manager
4. `NewBuildManagerInfoManager` — builds JSON manager identifier from `{Manager, Operation, APIVersion, Subresource}`; for Apply, omits APIVersion from identifier (stable across versions)
5. `NewCapManagersManager` — caps update managers at `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers=10`; merges oldest into "ancient-changes" versioned buckets
6. `NewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager` — skips tracking on create with probability `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability=1`; on first Apply with empty managed fields, bootstraps by calling Update with "before-first-apply" manager
7. `NewLastAppliedManager` — only active for manager="kubectl"; on conflict, checks `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation; if conflict fields are subset of last-applied fields (unchanged in live), force-applies
8. `NewLastAppliedUpdater` — only active for manager="kubectl"; after successful Apply, if annotation exists, updates it with new object state (excluding the annotation itself)
9. `NewVersionCheckManager` — validates applied object GVK matches expected GVK; passes through for Update

### structuredMergeManager (structuredmerge.go lines 43-195)
- `NewStructuredMergeManager`: creates `merge.Updater{Converter: newVersionConverter(...), IgnoreFilter: resetFields}`
- `NewCRDStructuredMergeManager`: uses `newCRDVersionConverter` (hub = exact hub GV, not group-relative)
- `Update()`: converts both objects to versioned, calls `ObjectToTyped` with `AllowDuplicates`, calls `f.updater.Update(liveTyped, newTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)`
- `Apply()`: validates patch GVK matches groupVersion; validates `managedFields == nil` in patch; converts live to versioned; calls `ObjectToTyped` on patch (no AllowDuplicates); calls `f.updater.Apply(liveTyped, patchTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager, force)`; on success: `TypedToObject` → `toVersioned` → `objectDefaulter.Default()` → `toUnversioned`

### versionConverter (versionconverter.go)
- `Convert(object, version)`: TypedToObject → ConvertToVersion(hub) → ConvertToVersion(target) → ObjectToTyped
- For built-ins: hub = `{Group: fromGroup, Version: hubVersion}` (group-relative hub)
- For CRDs: hub = exact hub GV
- `IsMissingVersionError`: returns true for `IsNotRegisteredError` OR `noCorrespondingTypeErr`

### FieldManager public API (fieldmanager.go)
- `Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls `decodeLiveOrNew`; `RemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)`; calls chain; `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls Update; on error logs via `atMostEverySecond.Do()`; clears managedFields; returns newObj
- `Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, manager, force)`: decodes managed from liveObj only; calls chain; on `merge.Conflicts` → `NewConflictError`; `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `isResetManagedFields`: true if managedFields is non-nil empty slice OR single empty entry

### decodeLiveOrNew logic
- Subresource: always use live object's managed fields (ignore request object's)
- Non-subresource: if request has `isResetManagedFields` → return `NewEmptyManaged()`; if request managed fields empty/invalid → fall back to live object's

### ManagedInterface (managedfields.go)
- `managedStruct{fields fieldpath.ManagedFields, times map[string]*metav1.Time}`
- `BuildManagerIdentifier`: JSON of `{Manager, Operation, APIVersion, Subresource}` with FieldsType/FieldsV1/Time zeroed; for Apply, APIVersion also zeroed
- `DecodeManagedFields`: validates Operation ∈ {Apply,Update}, APIVersion non-empty, FieldsType=="FieldsV1"
- `encodeManagedFields`: sorts by (Operation, Time.Unix, Manager, APIVersion, Subresource)

### TypeConverter implementations
- `typeConverter` (internal/typeconverter.go): `NewTypeConverter(openapiSpec, preserveUnknownFields)` → `schemaconv.ToSchemaFromOpenAPI` → `typed.Parser` → `indexModels` maps GVK→ParseableType via `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` extension
- `deducedTypeConverter`: uses `typed.DeducedParseableType`; all fields atomic; for CRDs without schema
- `schemeTypeConverter` (runtimetypeconverter.go): uses `scheme.ToOpenAPIDefinitionName(gvk)` → `parser.Type(name)`; new in 2025
- Public `NewTypeConverter(openapiSpec, preserveUnknownFields)` → internal; `NewDeducedTypeConverter()` → internal; `NewSchemeTypeConverter(scheme, parser)` → internal

### GvkParser (gvkparser.go)
- `GvkParser{gvks map[GVK]string, parser typed.Parser}`
- `NewGVKParser(models proto.Models, preserveUnknownFields)`: calls `schemaconv.ToSchemaWithPreserveUnknownFields`; reads `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` from proto model extensions
- `Type(gvk)`: returns `&parser.Type(typeName)`
- Used by apiserver to build TypeConverter from proto discovery models

### ExtractInto (extract.go)
- `ExtractInto(object, objectType, fieldManager, applyConfiguration, subresource)`:
  1. `toTyped(object, objectType)` → TypedValue
  2. `findManagedFields(accessor, fieldManager, subresource)` — finds entry where Manager==fieldManager AND Operation==Apply AND Subresource==subresource
  3. `fieldset.FromJSON(fieldsEntry.FieldsV1.Raw)`
  4. `typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves()).AsValue().Unstructured()`
  5. Injects kind/apiVersion if missing
  6. `runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(m, applyConfiguration)`
- Only works for Apply entries (not Update)
- Scale subresource caveat: selector paths differ, cannot use root object

### ScaleHandler (scalehandler.go)
- `ToSubresource()`: filters parent entries to those with known version mapping AND having replicas path; remaps to `spec.replicas` in `autoscaling/v1`
- `ToParent(scaleEntries)`: merges scale entries back; if manager not in scaleFields → "steal" replicas path (remove from parent entry); if manager in scaleFields → keep parent entry, delete from scaleFields; remaining scaleFields entries → translate back to resource-specific replicas path

### ConflictError (conflict.go)
- `NewConflictError(conflicts)`: creates `StatusError` with `CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict` causes; each cause has `Field=conflict.Path.String()`, `Message="conflict with <manager>"`
- `printManager`: unmarshals manager JSON; for Update includes APIVersion and time; for Apply just manager name (+ subresource if set)

### Admission (fieldmanager/admission.go)
- `NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap)`: wraps any admission.Interface
- `Admit()`: saves managedFields before admission; calls wrapped Admit; validates after; if invalid → restores pre-admission managedFields + adds warning via `warning.AddWarning`
- Warning format: `InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat`

### patch.go (apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go)
- Line 140-141: `ApplyCBORPatchType` gated on `CBORServingAndStorage` feature gate; returns 500 if reached without feature
- Line 372: non-apply patches → `UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, managerOrUserAgent(...))`
- Line 466: strategic/JSON/merge patches → `UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, managerOrUserAgent(...))`
- Lines 713-727: `isTooLargeError` retry: only for non-apply patch types; retries with `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` transformer injected

### installer.go (apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go lines 697-724)
- Checks storage for `ResetFieldsStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`) OR `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`)
- Mutual exclusion enforced
- Calls `fieldpath.NewExcludeFilterSetMap()` for former
- Passes `resetFieldsFilter` to `managedfields.NewDefaultFieldManager(...)`

### rest/rest.go
- `ResetFieldsStrategy`: `GetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`
- `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy`: `GetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`

### client-go applyconfigurations
- `internal/internal.go`: 15,478-line generated file; `Parser()` singleton returns `typed.Parser` from embedded `schemaYAML`
- `utils.go`: `ForKind(gvk)` giant switch returning typed `*XxxApplyConfiguration{}`
- `gentype/type.go`: `alsoApplier[T,C].Apply()` validates obj non-nil + name set; calls `apply.NewRequest`; chains `.UseProtobufAsDefaultIfPreferred().NamespaceIfScoped().Resource().Name().VersionedParams(&patchOpts).Do(ctx).Into(result)`
- `apply.go`: checks `ClientsAllowCBOR && ClientsPreferCBOR` feature gates; if both → `cbor.Marshal + ApplyCBORPatchType`; else `json.Marshal + ApplyYAMLPatchType`

### Constants
- `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers = 10`
- `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability = 1` (float32)
- `atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)`
- `LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration"`
- `ApplyYAMLPatchType = "application/apply-patch+yaml"`
- `ApplyCBORPatchType = "application/apply-patch+cbor"`

- `NewStructuredMergeManager`: creates `merge.Updater{Converter: newVersionConverter(...), IgnoreFilter: resetFields}`
- `NewCRDStructuredMergeManager`: uses `newCRDVersionConverter` (hub = exact hub GV, not group-relative)
- `Update()`: converts both objects to versioned, calls `ObjectToTyped` with `AllowDuplicates`, calls `f.updater.Update(liveTyped, newTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)`
- `Apply()`: validates patch GVK matches groupVersion; validates `managedFields == nil` in patch; converts live to versioned; calls `ObjectToTyped` on patch (no AllowDuplicates); calls `f.updater.Apply(liveTyped, patchTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager, force)`; on success: `TypedToObject` → `toVersioned` → `objectDefaulter.Default()` → `objectDefaulter.Default()` → `toUnversioned`

### versionConverter (versionconverter.go)
- `Convert(object, version)`: TypedToObject → ConvertToVersion(hub) → ConvertToVersion(target) → ObjectToTyped
- For built-ins: hub = `{Group: fromGroup, Version: hubVersion}` (group-relative hub)
- For CRDs: hub = exact hub GV
- `IsMissingVersionError`: returns true for `IsNotRegisteredError` OR `noCorrespondingTypeErr`

### FieldManager public API (fieldmanager.go)
- `Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls `decodeLiveOrNew`; `RemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)`; calls chain; `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls Update; on error logs via `atMostEverySecond.Do()`; clears managedFields; returns newObj
- `Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, manager, force)`: decodes managed from liveObj only; calls chain; on `merge.Conflicts` → `NewConflictError`; `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `isResetManagedFields`: true if managedFields is non-nil empty slice OR single empty entry

### decodeLiveOrNew logic
- Subresource: always use live object's managed fields (ignore request object's)
- Non-subresource: if request has `isResetManagedFields` → return `NewEmptyManaged()`; if request managed fields empty/invalid → fall back to live object's

### ManagedInterface (managedfields.go)
- `managedStruct{fields fieldpath.ManagedFields, times map[string]*metav1.Time}`
- `BuildManagerIdentifier`: marshals `{Manager, Operation, APIVersion, Subresource}` to JSON; for Apply omits APIVersion

### installer.go (apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go lines 697-724)
- Checks storage for `ResetFieldsStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`) OR `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy` (returns `map[APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`)
- Mutual exclusion enforced (error if both implemented)
- Calls `fieldpath.NewExcludeFilterSetMap()` for former
- Passes `resetFieldsFilter` to `managedfields.NewDefaultFieldManager(...)`

### rest/rest.go
- `ResetFieldsStrategy`: `GetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set`
- `ResetFieldsFilterStrategy`: `GetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter`

### Strategy example (pkg/registry/apps/daemonset/strategy.go lines 57-67)
- `daemonSetStrategy.GetResetFields()` returns `{"apps/v1": fieldpath.NewSet(fieldpath.MakePathOrDie("status"))}`
- Spec strategy resets status; status strategy resets spec — enforces subresource isolation

### patch.go handler flow
- Lines 138-140: `ApplyYAMLPatchType` / `ApplyCBORPatchType` → `applyPatcher`
- `applyPatcher.applyPatchToCurrentObject()` (lines 500-530): unmarshals patch to `unstructured.Unstructured`; calls `p.fieldManager.Apply(obj, patchObj, p.options.FieldManager, force)`; then validates strict if requested
- `applyPatcher.createNewObject()` (lines 532-537): creates empty object via `p.creater.New(p.kind)`; calls `applyPatchToCurrentObject` on it
- `jsonPatcher` / `smpPatcher`: call `fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, managerOrUserAgent(...))`
- Lines 713-727: `isTooLargeError` retry: only for non-apply patch types; retries with `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` transformer injected

### managerOrUserAgent / prefixFromUserAgent (create.go lines 259-283)
- If `manager != ""` → use as-is
- Else: take prefix before first `/` from User-Agent; strip non-printable chars; truncate to `FieldManagerMaxLength=128`

### client-go applyconfigurations
- `internal/internal.go`: 15,478-line generated file; `Parser()` singleton returns `typed.Parser` from embedded `schemaYAML`
- `utils.go`: `ForKind(gvk)` giant switch returning typed `*XxxApplyConfiguration{}`
- `gentype/type.go`: `alsoApplier[T,C].Apply()` validates obj non-nil + name set; calls `apply.NewRequest`; chains `.UseProtobufAsDefaultIfPreferred().NamespaceIfScoped().Resource().Name().VersionedParams(&patchOpts).Do(ctx).Into(result)`
- `alsoApplier[T,C].ApplyStatus()`: same but adds `.SubResource("status")`
- `apply.go`: checks `ClientsAllowCBOR && ClientsPreferCBOR` feature gates; if both → `cbor.Marshal + ApplyCBORPatchType`; else `json.Marshal + ApplyYAMLPatchType`

### code-generator (applyconfiguration-gen)
- `applyconfiguration.go`: generates struct with all-pointer fields; `With<Field>` builder methods; `Extract<Type>From()` function using `ExtractInto` + `Parser().Type(openAPIType)`; `ManagedFields` field is blocklisted (line 144)
- `internal.go` generator: emits `Parser()` singleton with embedded `schemaYAML` (OpenAPI schema trimmed to referenced types only)
- `util.go` generator: emits `ForKind(gvk)` switch + `NewTypeConverter(scheme)`
- `openapi.go`: reads OpenAPI schema file; builds GVK→openAPIType map; trims to referenced types; validates via `toValidatedModels`
- Constructor variants: namespaced (`WithName+WithNamespace+WithKind+WithAPIVersion`), non-namespaced (no namespace), TypeMeta-only, bare

### Constants
- `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers = 10`
- `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability = 1` (float32)
- `atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)`
- `LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration"`
- `ApplyYAMLPatchType = "application/apply-patch+yaml"`
- `ApplyCBORPatchType = "application/apply-patch+cbor"`
- `FieldManagerMaxLength = 128`

Must cover: subsystem purpose/boundaries/dependencies, key components, end-to-end flow with repo evidence, failure modes/tradeoffs, extension points, and file map. Must cite concrete file paths, explain behavior/interactions, include operational implications section. No fabricated code/files.

- Write `documentation.md` (all research complete, ready to write)

CURRENT_STATE:
- No documentation.md exists yet
- All source reading complete — ready to write

---

## Complete Source Knowledge Base (Verified Final Pass)

### FieldManager Public API (`fieldmanager.go` / `internal/fieldmanager.go`)
- `NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, objectCreater, kind, hub, subresource, resetFields)` → calls `NewStructuredMergeManager` then `NewDefaultFieldManager` (internal)
- `NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(...)` → calls `NewCRDStructuredMergeManager` then `NewDefaultFieldManager` (internal)
- `ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)` → public validation entry point
- `FieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: `decodeLiveOrNew` → `RemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)` → chain → `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `FieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj, manager)`: calls Update; on error logs via `atMostEverySecond.Do()`; clears managedFields; returns newObj
- `FieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, manager, force)`: decodes managed from liveObj only → chain → on `merge.Conflicts` → `NewConflictError` → `EncodeObjectManagedFields`
- `isResetManagedFields`: true if managedFields is non-nil empty slice OR single empty `ManagedFieldsEntry{}`
- `decodeLiveOrNew`: subresource → always use live; non-subresource: if `isResetManagedFields` → `NewEmptyManaged()`; if empty/invalid → fall back to live

### Manager Decorator Chain (innermost → outermost, from `internal/fieldmanager.go` NewDefaultFieldManager)
1. `structuredMergeManager` (core SMD logic)
2. `NewStripMetaManager` — strips: apiVersion, kind, metadata, metadata.name, metadata.namespace, metadata.creationTimestamp, metadata.selfLink, metadata.uid, metadata.clusterName, metadata.generation, metadata.managedFields, metadata.resourceVersion
3. `NewManagedFieldsUpdater` — uses sentinel manager "current-operation"; detects changes; merges with previous same-manager entries; sets `time.Now().UTC()`
4. `NewBuildManagerInfoManager` — builds JSON `{Manager, Operation, APIVersion, Subresource}`; for Apply omits APIVersion (stable across versions); empty manager → "unknown"
5. `NewCapManagersManager` — caps update managers at `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers=10`; merges oldest into "ancient-changes" versioned buckets by APIVersion
6. `NewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager` — on Update: if managed empty + UID empty (create) → skip with prob `1-DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability`; if managed empty + UID set (update) → skip; on Apply: if managed empty → bootstrap via Update with "before-first-apply" manager
7. `NewLastAppliedManager` — only active for manager="kubectl"; on Apply conflict: checks `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation; computes allowed conflict set (fields in last-applied that are unchanged in live); if all conflicts are subset → force-applies
8. `NewLastAppliedUpdater` — only active for manager="kubectl"; after successful Apply: if annotation exists → updates it with new object state (excluding annotation itself)
9. `NewVersionCheckManager` — on Apply: validates `appliedObj.GVK == f.gvk`; passes through for Update

### structuredMergeManager (`internal/structuredmerge.go`)
- `NewStructuredMergeManager`: `merge.Updater{Converter: newVersionConverter(...), IgnoreFilter: resetFields}`
- `NewCRDStructuredMergeManager`: `merge.Updater{Converter: newCRDVersionConverter(...), IgnoreFilter: resetFields}`
- `Update()`: `toVersioned(newObj)` + `toVersioned(liveObj)` → `ObjectToTyped` with `AllowDuplicates` for both → `f.updater.Update(liveTyped, newTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)` → returns newObj (unmodified)
- `Apply()`: validates patch GVK == groupVersion; validates `managedFields == nil`; `toVersioned(liveObj)` → `ObjectToTyped(patchObj)` (no AllowDuplicates) + `ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, AllowDuplicates)` → `f.updater.Apply(...)` → `TypedToObject` → `toVersioned` → `objectDefaulter.Default()` → `toUnversioned`
- `toVersioned`: `objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.groupVersion)`
- `toUnversioned`: `objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.hubVersion)`

### versionConverter (`internal/versionconverter.go`)
- Built-in: `hubGetter = func(from GV) GV { return {Group: from.Group, Version: h.Version} }` (group-relative hub)
- CRD: `hubGetter = func(from GV) GV { return h }` (exact hub GV)
- `Convert(object, version)`: `TypedToObject` → `ConvertToVersion(hub)` → `ConvertToVersion(target)` → `ObjectToTyped`
- `IsMissingVersionError`: `IsNotRegisteredError(err) || isNoCorrespondingTypeError(err)`

### TypeConverter implementations (`internal/typeconverter.go`, `internal/runtimetypeconverter.go`)
- `typeConverter`: GVK→ParseableType map built from OpenAPI spec via `schemaconv.ToSchemaFromOpenAPI`; `ObjectToTyped` dispatches on Unstructured vs structured
- `deducedTypeConverter`: uses `typed.DeducedParseableType` (all fields atomic); for CRDs without schema
- `schemeTypeConverter` (new, 2025): uses `scheme.ToOpenAPIDefinitionName(gvk)` + `parser.Type(name)`
- `GvkParser` (`gvkparser.go`): built from `proto.Models` via `schemaconv.ToSchemaWithPreserveUnknownFields`; maps GVK→typeName

### ManagedFields Encoding/Decoding (`internal/managedfields.go`)
- `ManagedInterface`: `Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields` + `Times() map[string]*metav1.Time`
- `DecodeManagedFields`: validates Operation (Apply|Update), APIVersion non-empty, FieldsType="FieldsV1"; calls `BuildManagerIdentifier`
- `BuildManagerIdentifier`: JSON marshal of entry with FieldsType=nil, FieldsV1=nil, Time=nil; for Apply: APIVersion="" (stable)
- `encodeManagedFields`: iterates fields; calls `encodeManagerVersionedSet`; adds times; sorts by (Operation, Time, Manager, APIVersion, Subresource)
- `RemoveObjectManagedFields`: sets managedFields=nil on object
- `EncodeObjectManagedFields`: encodes and sets on object

### Conflict Handling (`internal/conflict.go`)
- `NewConflictError(conflicts)`: creates `StatusError` with `CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict` causes; calls `errors.NewApplyConflict`
- `printManager`: unmarshals manager JSON; formats as `"name" using v1/apps at <time>` for updates, `"name"` for apply

### ScaleHandler (`scalehandler.go`)
- `ToSubresource()`: filters parent entries to those with replicas path; remaps to `autoscaling/v1` + `spec.replicas`
- `ToParent(scaleEntries)`: merges scale entries back; handles "steal" of replicas path from parent

### ExtractInto (`extract.go`)
- Finds managed fields entry by (manager, operation=Apply, subresource)
- `typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves())` → unstructured → `FromUnstructured` into applyConfiguration
- Sets kind/apiVersion if missing

### Admission Integration (`handlers/fieldmanager/admission.go`)
- `NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap)`: wraps admission controller
- `Admit()`: saves managedFields before admission; calls wrapped Admit; if `ValidateManagedFields` fails → restores original + adds warning `InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat`

### HTTP Handler Integration
**create.go** (line 199): `scope.FieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, obj, managerOrUserAgent(...))` before admission; liveObj = fresh empty object from `scope.Creater.New(scope.Kind)`

**update.go** (lines 155-234): `shouldUpdateManagedFields=true`; transformer calls `UpdateNoErrors`; on `isTooLargeError`: `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` + `shouldUpdateManagedFields=false` + retry

**patch.go** (lines 697-728): transformers = `[applyPatch, applyAdmission, dedupOwnerReferencesTransformer]`; on `isTooLargeError` for non-Apply patch types: adds transformer that calls `accessor.SetManagedFields(nil)` + retry; Apply patch types do NOT get this fallback

**installer.go** (lines 685-724): routes `PATCH` with `application/apply-patch+yaml` or `application/apply-patch+cbor` to `applyPatcher`

### applyPatcher flow (`patch.go`)
- Decodes patch body as unstructured
- Calls `scope.FieldManager.Apply(liveObj, patchObj, manager, force)`
- On `merge.Conflicts` → HTTP 409 Conflict with field-level details

### client-go Apply path
- `apply.go` (`util/apply/apply.go`): checks `ClientsAllowCBOR && ClientsPreferCBOR` feature gates; if both → `cbor.Marshal + ApplyCBORPatchType`; else `json.Marshal + ApplyYAMLPatchType`; returns `client.Patch(pt).Body(body)`
- `gentype/type.go` `alsoApplier.Apply()`: validates obj non-nil + name set; calls `apply.NewRequest`; chains `.UseProtobufAsDefaultIfPreferred().NamespaceIfScoped().Resource().Name().VersionedParams(&patchOpts).Do(ctx).Into(result)`
- `alsoApplier.ApplyStatus()`: same + `.SubResource("status")`

### applyconfiguration-gen (code-generator)
- Generates all-pointer-field structs with `With<Field>` builder methods
- `Extract<Type>From()` uses `ExtractInto` + `Parser().Type(openAPIType)`
- `internal.go`: emits `Parser()` singleton with embedded `schemaYAML`
- `util.go`: emits `ForKind(gvk)` switch + `NewTypeConverter(scheme)`
- `ManagedFields` field is blocklisted (line 144 of applyconfiguration.go)
- Constructor variants: namespaced, non-namespaced, TypeMeta-only, bare

### Constants
- `DefaultMaxUpdateManagers = 10`
- `DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability = 1` (float32)
- `atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)`
- `LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration"`
- `ApplyYAMLPatchType = "application/apply-patch+yaml"`
- `ApplyCBORPatchType = "application/apply-patch+cbor"`
- `FieldManagerMaxLength = 128`
- `beforeApplyManagerName = "before-first-apply"`
- `oldUpdatesManagerName = "ancient-changes"`
- `sentinel manager = "current-operation"` (in ManagedFieldsUpdater)

---
